refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these obligations are separated. The refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need without implying how it is done or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can identify and specify performance requirements based solely on the worksite mission context without reference to implementation details.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the history of performance requirements and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository to maintain history of baselines and calibration updates
Develop a validation process to ensure derived performance requirements comply with safety plans and operational policies
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements

potential_sources: Tight integration between mission context inputs and performance requirement outputs
Dependency on safety plan and operational policies for validation
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical model that isolates the translation process, supporting independence. Validation and history maintenance are downstream concerns that can be modularized separately.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and completeness of worksite mission context data
Risk that safety and operational policies may change frequently requiring model updates
Assumes version control and documentation processes are implemented in separate modules
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the validation and history maintenance functions into distinct DPs or IMs to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and data processing components responsible for translating worksite mission context into performance requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data about the worksite and vehicle status to inform the logical model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends performance requirements and task directives derived from the logical model to other system modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external systems and remote operators, supporting data exchange necessary for mission context interpretation.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the logical model is central to the NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since most related data and processing reside here.
Vehicle module focuses on physical control and actuation; containing the logical model here would cause significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation handles remote control aspects; partial relevance but containing the logical model here would diffuse logic across control and mission planning.
Safety Services focus on safety monitoring and alerts; containing the logical model here is weak and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for the logical model translating mission context into performance requirements, as it centralizes system logic and minimizes diffusion across modules.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth within NGHE Solution if the logical model expands significantly, risking module bloat.
Dependency on accurate and timely telemetry data; interface failures could impact model accuracy.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by defining a model that translates the worksite mission context into performance requirements, directly fulfilling the need to derive performance requirements from mission context.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the logical model is well centralized within this module. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary. Interfaces are well defined and support necessary data flows without scattering the DP across multiple modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and maintaining history of baselines/calibration updates within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the logical model to the NGHE Solution module is clear and aligns with the functional requirement, with well-defined interfaces supporting data input and output.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to prevent module bloat as the logical model expands.
Implement robust error handling and fallback mechanisms for telemetry data interface failures to maintain model accuracy and reliability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows high containment and low diffusion, with clear allocation and feasible verification. Given the high safety criticality, further decomposition into next-level sub-FRs is prudent to ensure detailed coverage and manage complexity effectively. This will also help monitor and control module bloat and implement robust error handling as recommended.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive sub-FRs that specify detailed performance requirements from distinct aspects of the worksite mission context, such as environmental conditions, operational constraints, and telemetry data processing.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to prevent module bloat as the logical model expands, and implement robust error handling and fallback mechanisms for telemetry data interface failures to maintain model accuracy and reliability.

assumptions: The current FR and DP mapping is logically sound and verified.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary with high containment and low diffusion.
Verification methods are feasible and traceable within the current module.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module leading to module bloat.
Telemetry data interface failures impacting model accuracy and reliability.
evidence_needed: Detailed sub-FRs derived from the worksite mission context.
Metrics on module complexity and interface robustness over time.
